what_it_does: Locks the D-1 keying for a multi-space addressing and embedding-axis registry, defines each semantic axis as one (embedding model × embedding way) row, and unblocks recall indexing on the first served axis.
observations: YAML frontmatter marks it as fabric-derived, authored by ch-al7jdfdr, dated 2026-06-14, and verified via Tim D1+D6's delegated call.
A unit's address is composed of co-equal coordinate spaces: provenance (exchange://<sid>/<i>) as canonical spine, temporal (ts), file-position, structural/provenance sub-axes, and semantic axes.
Semantic axes are not a single axis but one co-equal axis per (embedding model × embedding way), held in an extensible registry.
Registry row schema is {id, model, way, endpoint, dim, metric, instruction?, status: served|planned}.
First populated axis is pplx-ctx-4b-docs using perplexity-ai/pplx-embed-context-v1-4b documents-mode at 127.0.0.1:8007, dim 2560, cosine metric.
Recall queries carry an axis selector with a default primary served axis and optional multi-axis fusion via RRF.
Golden rule: index and query within the same axis; provenance/temporal/file-position filters apply across any axis.
D-1 gate is declared resolved and indexing on pplx-ctx-4b-docs should proceed now, with other axes added lazily as they are served.
Registry is intended to be file-discovered under embedding_axes/ and is described as reversible/re-keyable.
standouts: Semantic coordinate space is one axis per (embedding model × embedding way), not a single semantic axis.
Provenance (exchange://<sid>/<i>) is the canonical, re-embed-stable join key across all axes.
D-1 indexing gate is resolved by a delegated call and unblocked immediately for pplx-ctx-4b-docs.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for authorship, date, trust, verification, and related-file links.
Follows a registry pattern analogous to roles/ and platforms/.
Records delegated authority explicitly (Tim D1+D6: 'your call on the registry shape').
Uses status field served|planned to distinguish available vs future axes.
Advocates lazy, incremental backfill rather than waiting for all axes.
concerns: Endpoint 127.0.0.1:8007 is hardcoded with no health or verification mechanism described.
'served' status is asserted without in-file evidence of runtime checks.
Lazy backfill strategy, versioning, and trigger conditions are unspecified.
No schema validation or required-field enforcement is described for registry rows.
The phrase 'semantic NOT one axis' could be misread without the surrounding model explanation.
notes: This file is the lead's delegated lock-in of the embedding-axis registry shape, resolving the D-1 addressing gate so recall indexing can start on pplx-ctx-4b-docs while remaining extensible.
questions: What component consumes the file-discovered registry under embedding_axes/?
How is lazy backfill triggered, scheduled, and versioned when new axes are added?
What RRF parameters and score normalization are used for multi-axis fusion?
How is the 'primary served axis' selected when multiple axes are served?
How are instruction-steered variants (e.g., Qwen3-Embedding) represented in the registry row?
What exactly defines segment/compaction generation as a structural sub-axis?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual: it defines the registry model and declares the D-1 gate resolved to unblock indexing.
apparent_themes: Multi-space addressing with provenance as canonical identity
Embedding axes as extensible registry entries
Per-axis retrieval with optional cross-axis fusion
Incremental build: start with one served axis, add others lazily
intention_signals: Establish a stable, extensible keying scheme for recall so indexing can proceed immediately on one served embedding axis, while allowing additional model/way combinations to be added later and joined via provenance.
novelty: Treats each (embedding model × embedding way) as a separate co-equal semantic coordinate space in an extensible registry, rather than collapsing semantics into a single axis, and uses provenance as the cross-axis join key.
contribution: A concrete registry schema and addressing model that separates stable provenance/temporal/structural coordinates from per-embedding semantic axes, plus a decision to begin indexing on one served axis without waiting for all axes to be available.
summary_for_embedding: Embedding-axis registry specification that defines a unit's multi-space address as provenance, temporal, file-position, structural, and one semantic axis per embedding model/way, with the first served axis pplx-ctx-4b-docs unblocking D-1 indexing.
intention_for_embedding: To lock the keying shape for multi-space recall indexing and authorize immediate indexing on the currently served axis while keeping the registry extensible.
